goal: is_injective_fn[G, H](f.hom) = forall(x0: G, x1: G) { f.hom(x0) = f.hom(x1) implies x0 = x1 }
proof: function[T0, T1](x0: T0 -> T1) { forall(x1: T0, x2: T0) { x0(x2) != x0(x1) or x2 = x1 } = is_injective_fn[T0, T1](x0) }[G, H](f.hom)

goal: is_trivial_group_hom_kernel[G, H](f) = forall(x0: G) { f.hom(x0) = H.1 implies x0 = G.1 }
proof: function[T0: Group, T1: Group](x0: GroupHom[T0, T1]) { forall(x1: T0) { T1.1 != x0.hom(x1) or T0.1 = x1 } = is_trivial_group_hom_kernel[T0, T1](x0) }[G, H](f)

(forall(x0: G) { H.1 != f.hom(x0) or G.1 = x0 } = true)

let w2: G satisfy { f.hom(w0) = f.hom(w2) and w0 != w2 }

goal: is_injective_fn[A, B](f.hom) = forall(x0: A, x1: A) { f.hom(x0) = f.hom(x1) implies x0 = x1 }

goal: is_trivial_ring_hom_kernel[F, E](field_hom_to_ring_hom[F, E](f)) = forall(x0: F) { field_hom_to_ring_hom[F, E](f).hom(x0) = E.0 implies x0 = F.0 }
